title: platycerium bifurcatum subsp. bifurcatum
intro: Platycerium bifurcatum subsp. bifurcatum, also known as the veined staghorn fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to Australia and Southeast Asia. This plant is a type of epiphyte that grows on the surface of other plants, such as trees, without deriving nutrients from them. The veined staghorn fern has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are often used to treat various health issues. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin irritations, and to promote overall well-being.
